title: melampyrum arvense subsp. elatius
intro: Melampyrum arvense subsp. elatius, also known as the tall cow wheat or cow wheat, is a rare plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Melampyrum arvense subsp. elatius has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, but further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and optimal uses.
